an7.53:1.2: that on one occasion Ven. Sāriputta & Ven. Mahā Moggallāna were on a wandering tour of the Southern Mountains with a large Saṅgha of monks.
an7.53:1.3: And on that occasion, the female lay follower Veḷukaṇṭakin Nandamātar, having risen as night approached dawn, chanted the Pārāyana. And on that occasion the great [deva] king Vessavaṇṇa3 was traveling from north to south on some business or other.
an7.53:2.1: He heard the female lay follower Nandamātar chanting the Pārāyana, and on hearing her, stood waiting for the end of her chant.
an7.53:2.2: Then the female lay follower Nandamātar, having chanted the Pārāyana, fell silent.
an7.53:3.1: Then the great king Vessavaṇṇa, knowing that her chant was ended, applauded her:
an7.53:3.4: “But who’s there, O One of the Auspicious Face?”
an7.53:3.7: “Excellent, O One of the Auspicious Face!
an7.53:3.9: Then let this Dhamma sequence that I’ve chanted be your guest gift.”
an7.53:4.2: And let this also be my guest gift.
an7.53:4.3: This morning a Saṅgha of monks headed by Sāriputta & Moggallāna will be coming to Veḷukaṇṭaka before breakfast.
an7.53:4.4: Having served them, dedicate the offering to me.
an7.53:4.5: That will also be my guest gift.”
an7.53:4.6: So Nandamātar, as the night was ending, prepared exquisite staple & non-staple foods in her home.
an7.53:4.7: Then the Saṅgha of monks headed by Sāriputta & Moggallāna arrived at Veḷukaṇṭaka before breakfast.
an7.53:4.8: Nandamātar addressed a man, “Come, my good man.
an7.53:4.9: Having gone to the monastery, announce the time (of the meal) to the Saṅgha of monks, (saying,) ‘It’s time, venerable sirs.
an7.53:5.1: The meal is ready at Lady Nandamātar’s home.’”
an7.53:5.2: Responding, “As you say, lady,” to Nandamātar, the man went to the monastery and announced the time to the Saṅgha of monks, “It’s time, venerable sirs.
an7.53:5.3: The meal is ready at Lady Nandamātar’s home.”
an7.53:6.1: Then early in the morning, having adjusted their under robes and carrying their robes & bowls, the Saṅgha of monks headed by Sāriputta & Moggallāna went to Nandamātar’s home.
an7.53:6.2: On arrival, they sat down on seats laid out.
an7.53:6.3: Nandamātar, with her own hands, served & satisfied the Saṅgha of monks headed by Sāriputta & Moggallāna with exquisite staple & non-staple food.
an7.53:6.4: Then, when Ven. Sāriputta had finished his meal and had rinsed his bowl & hands, Nandamātar, taking a low seat, sat to one side.
an7.53:6.5: As she was sitting there, Ven. Sāriputta said to her, “Nandamātar, who informed you of the arrival of the Saṅgha of monks?”
an7.53:6.6: “Just now, venerable sir, after having risen as night approached dawn and having chanted the Pārāyana, I fell silent.
an7.53:6.7: Then the great king Vessavaṇṇa, knowing that my chant was ended, applauded me:
an7.53:6.10: “‘But who’s there, O One of the Auspicious Face?’
an7.53:8.1: “‘Excellent, O One of the Auspicious Face!
an7.53:8.3: Then let this Dhamma sequence that I’ve chanted be your guest gift.’
an7.53:8.5: And let this also be my guest gift.
an7.53:8.6: Tomorrow a Saṅgha of monks headed by Sāriputta & Moggallāna will be coming to Veḷukaṇṭaka before breakfast.
an7.53:8.7: Having served them, dedicate the offering to me.
an7.53:9.1: That will also be my guest gift.’ So in other words, may the merit of this gift be for the happiness of the great king Vessavaṇṇa.”
an7.53:9.3: It’s astounding, that you would converse face to face with the great king Vessavaṇṇa, the young deva of such great power & such great might!”
an7.53:9.4: “I don’t have only that amazing & astounding quality, venerable sir.
an7.53:9.5: I have another amazing & astounding quality.
an7.53:10.1: The rulers seized & abducted him on some pretext and had him executed.
an7.53:10.2: But when the boy had been arrested or was being arrested, when he had been imprisoned or was being imprisoned, when he had been killed or was being killed, I don’t recall any alteration of my mind.”
an7.53:10.4: It’s astounding, that you can purify even as little as the arising of a thought.”4
an7.53:10.5: “I don’t have only that amazing & astounding quality, venerable sir.
an7.53:11.1: I have another amazing & astounding quality.
an7.53:11.2: When my husband died, he reappeared in the yakkha realm.
an7.53:11.3: He appeared to me in his earlier bodily form, but I don’t recall, from that cause, any alteration of my mind.”
an7.53:12.1: It’s astounding, that you can purify even as little as the arising of a thought.”
an7.53:12.2: “I don’t have only that amazing & astounding quality, venerable sir.
an7.53:12.3: I have another amazing & astounding quality.
an7.53:12.4: I was given in marriage to my young husband when I was a young girl, but I don’t recall ever transgressing against him in mind, much less in body.”
an7.53:12.6: It’s astounding, that you can purify even as little as the arising of a thought.”
an7.53:12.7: “I don’t have only that amazing & astounding quality, venerable sir.
an7.53:13.1: I have another amazing & astounding quality.
an7.53:13.2: Since declaring myself a lay follower, I don’t recall ever deliberately overstepping any training rule.”
an7.53:14.1: “I don’t have only that amazing & astounding quality, venerable sir.
an7.53:14.2: I have another amazing & astounding quality.
an7.53:14.3: To whatever extent I want, quite secluded from sensuality, secluded from unskillful qualities, I enter & remain in the first jhāna:
an7.53:15.1: rapture & pleasure born of seclusion, accompanied by directed thought & evaluation.
an7.53:15.2: With the stilling of directed thoughts & evaluations, I enter & remain in the second jhāna:
an7.53:15.3: rapture & pleasure born of concentration, unification of awareness free from directed thought & evaluation—internal assurance.
an7.53:15.4: With the fading of rapture, I remain equanimous, mindful, & alert, and sense pleasure with the body.
